title: NOC 41404 – Health Policy Researchers, Consultants and Program Officers | GTR Immigration
description: Health Policy Researchers, Consultants and Program Officers under NOC 41404 are strategic professionals who develop, evaluate, and manage public health policies and healthcare programs across Canada. Classified as TEER 1 (Professional Occupation), this role is essential to strengthening Canada’s healthcare system, improving public health standards, and ensuring regulatory compliance. These professionals work in government departments, hospitals, research institutes, consulting firms, universities, NGOs, and international health organizations.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community and government servicesTEER1 – Occupations usually require a university degreeMajor Group41 – Professional occupations in law, education, social, community and government servicesSub-Major Group414 – Professional occupations in government servicesMinor Group4140 – Policy and program researchers, consultants and officersNOC Code41404

common_job_titles: Child health care programs planning officer
duties: Health Policy Researchers and Program Officers may:
Assist in developing government health policy
Review research literature and conduct interviews
Collect and analyze statistical health data
Advise senior officials on health regulation and financing
Design and implement health programs and projects
Maintain and manage health information databases
Respond to policy and program information requests
Prepare reports, newsletters, and public communication materials
Represent organizations before policymakers and media
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s degree or diploma in:Health sciencePublic administrationHospital administrationSocial scienceRecreation administration
Graduate degree in health, public policy, or administration may be required
Several years of experience in healthcare, community services, or policy roles are usually required
Foreign-trained professionals may require credential assessment for immigration purposes.
salary_prospects: Health Policy Researchers and Program Officers typically earn between:
$41,700 – $107,800 per year,depending on experience, specialization, and region.
Senior health consultants and policy directors may earn higher compensation.
job_outlook: Mental health and addiction strategy growth
Healthcare policy remains a high-priority sector across Canada.
labour_market_demand: Professionals under NOC 41404 are employed in:
Mental health and addiction programs expansion
Health data analytics and regulatory development
Career stability in public and institutional sectors
Eligibility for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs
Professionals with backgrounds in public health, health administration, epidemiology, or healthcare management are particularly competitive.
pathways: Professionals under this NOC may qualify for:
Advanced education and healthcare experience strengthen immigration competitiveness.
